products.name: Devolutions Hub Business
lab_concepts: Hub Business Vault Architecture & Role-Based Access
SSO & MFA Integration with Active Directory
recommendation: Deploy a Windows Hyper-V VM with Remote Desktop Manager and Devolutions Workspace pre-installed as the primary lab environment, and use the Hub Business REST API to provision a per-learner vault and user account at lab launch. This hybrid approach is the right choice because Hub Business is cloud-hosted SaaS — the client-side experience (where learners spend most of their time) lives on the Windows VM, while the API handles the cloud-side per-learner isolation. Devolutions Server (self-hosted) is worth evaluating as an alternative: it installs directly on a Hyper-V VM and eliminates the SaaS API dependency entirely, potentially simplifying the lab build.

The Hub Business REST API can validate vault configuration state, user permission assignments, and credential entries programmatically — enabling Performance-Based Testing for admin tasks such as 'configure least-privilege vault access' or 'enable MFA for all vault users.' PowerShell scripting (supported by Devolutions' own PowerShell Universal product) can supplement API scoring for client-side validation. Together these support rich, automated scoring without falling back to MCQ.

The key technical question that blocks or unblocks this lab build is whether Devolutions will provide NFR or training-tier Hub Business tenants for lab use at scale, and whether the REST API supports bulk vault/user creation without rate limits. The champion should direct this question to Devolutions' partnerships or product team: 'Can we provision isolated Hub Business vaults per learner via your REST API for training lab use, and do you offer NFR or training-tier licenses for lab environments at scale?' The Devolutions Academy team is the most likely internal champion — they already own the training brand and have motivation to add hands-on labs.

Devolutions Academy is the natural home for these labs — the company already has a training brand and a free learning hub. Labs would directly accelerate customer onboarding and reduce time-to-value for new Hub Business deployments, addressing the documented adoption risk of credential sprawl when teams don't fully adopt a centralized vault. A second-phase opportunity is partner/reseller enablement: the reseller network needs technical depth to position Hub Business against CyberArk and BeyondTrust, and hands-on labs are the most effective way to build that depth.

Devolutions has no confirmed certification program — a notable gap compared to CyberArk, BeyondTrust, and 1Password, all of which operate formal credential management certifications. A Devolutions Hub Business certification backed by Performance-Based Testing would differentiate the product in a crowded PAM market and create a recurring consumption motion. This is worth raising with the Academy team as a strategic opportunity, not just a lab delivery conversation.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Hub Business is cloud-hosted SaaS. The lab environment is a Windows VM (Hyper-V) with the RDM and Workspace clients pre-installed, connecting to a per-learner Hub Business vault provisioned via the Devolutions REST API. The Hyper-V client VM is clean and straightforward; the cloud-side provisioning adds a Sandbox API dependency that introduces moderate build complexity.

evidence.claim: **Client-Side VM Path | Strength:** The primary lab experience runs on a Windows VM (Hyper-V) with Remote Desktop Manager and Devolutions Workspace pre-installed, connecting to a Hub Business cloud vault. The client software installs cleanly on Windows — the VM image is the lab environment for all client-side tasks. — Devolutions product page. This is the standard Hyper-V path; no exotic dependencies on the client side.

**REST API Coverage | Opportunity:** Devolutions Hub Business exposes a documented REST API covering vault creation, user management, role assignment, and credential operations — enabling per-learner Hub Business environment provisioning alongside the Hyper-V client VM. — Devolutions documentation. This API is the mechanism for provisioning the cloud-side component of each learner's lab environment.

**Multi-Component Lab Design | Opportunity:** A realistic Hub Business lab involves at least two components — a Windows client VM (RDM/Workspace) and the Hub Business cloud backend — plus optionally an Active Directory VM for SSO/MFA integration scenarios. This multi-component topology is a Skillable strength that competitors struggle to replicate. — Devolutions product ecosystem documentation. Enables richer, more realistic admin scenarios.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: Devolutions Hub Business exposes a REST API covering user creation, role assignment, and vault access management — enabling per-learner identity provisioning. Training or NFR license availability is not self-serve confirmed and likely requires vendor engagement. Learner isolation via per-vault provisioning is indicated by the API surface.

evidence.claim: **User Provisioning API | Strength:** The Hub Business REST API supports creating users, assigning roles, and managing vault access programmatically — enabling automated per-learner identity setup without manual admin intervention. — Devolutions documentation. This is the core mechanism for per-learner lab access provisioning.

**Per-Vault Isolation | Strength:** Hub Business supports multiple independent vaults with separate user memberships and permissions — each learner can be provisioned into a dedicated vault, providing genuine per-learner isolation without shared state. — Devolutions Hub Business architecture documentation. Isolation is achievable via API-driven vault provisioning.

**NFR License Path | Risk:** Devolutions offers a free trial and a 'Proof of Concept' program for 100+ user evaluations, suggesting a path to training/NFR licenses. However, no self-serve NFR or lab-tier license is explicitly documented — vendor engagement will be required to negotiate training-use terms at scale. — Devolutions homepage (POC program reference). This is a build-time dependency that requires early vendor conversation.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: The Hub Business REST API can validate vault configuration state, user permissions, and credential entries programmatically. Devolutions' strong PowerShell culture (PowerShell Universal product) suggests script-based scoring is also viable. Together these support Performance-Based Testing scenarios for admin configuration tasks.

evidence.claim: **REST API State Validation | Opportunity:** The Hub Business REST API can query vault configuration state, user permission assignments, and credential entries — enabling programmatic validation of learner-completed admin tasks such as 'create a vault with least-privilege roles' or 'configure MFA policy'. — Devolutions documentation. Supports rich Performance-Based Testing scenarios for credential management administration.

**PowerShell Scripting Culture | Opportunity:** Devolutions produces PowerShell Universal as a dedicated automation product, indicating deep PowerShell integration across the product suite. PowerShell modules for Hub Business administration are likely available, enabling script-based scoring of client-side and server-side configuration tasks. — Devolutions PowerShell Universal product page. Strengthens scoring coverage beyond the REST API alone.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: The Hyper-V client VM tears down automatically via snapshot revert. The Hub Business cloud component requires API calls to delete per-learner users and vaults. Orphan risk exists if API teardown calls fail or are incomplete, requiring monitoring and retry logic in the lab teardown script.

evidence.claim: **Automatic VM Teardown | Strength:** The Hyper-V client VM tears down automatically via Skillable snapshot revert at lab end — no manual cleanup required for the client-side environment. This covers the majority of the lab footprint. — Skillable platform capability. Standard datacenter teardown applies to all Hyper-V labs.

**API-Driven Cloud Cleanup | Opportunity:** The Hub Business REST API supports deleting users and vaults programmatically, enabling automated teardown of the cloud-side lab component at session end. Lab teardown scripts can call the API to remove per-learner vaults and user accounts. — Devolutions documentation. Teardown is automatable but requires explicit scripting as a lab build task.

**Cloud Orphan Risk | Risk:** If API teardown calls fail (network error, rate limit, timeout), per-learner Hub Business vaults and user accounts may persist in the cloud tenant, accumulating orphaned resources over time. Lab teardown scripts must include error handling and retry logic, and periodic audits of the Hub Business tenant are advisable. — Inferred from SaaS API teardown patterns. Standard risk for any cloud-component lab with API-driven cleanup.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Hub Business requires meaningful configuration depth: vault architecture design, user and group permission hierarchies, SSO and MFA policy configuration, PAM integration, and audit log management. Multiple distinct roles (Hub admin, vault manager, end user, security auditor) create role-based lab scenarios. The product is not trivially learned by watching — hands-on practice is required to internalize permission models and policy interactions.

evidence.claim: **Vault Architecture Depth | Strength:** Hub Business requires configuring vault hierarchies, user and group permission models, role-based access control, SSO/MFA policy enforcement, and PAM integration — a multi-layer configuration surface that cannot be learned by reading documentation alone. — Devolutions Hub Business product documentation. Hands-on practice is essential to internalize permission inheritance and policy interactions.

**Multi-Role Admin Surface | Strength:** Hub Business supports distinct roles — Hub administrator, vault manager, end user, and security auditor — each with different configuration responsibilities and access scopes. Role-based lab scenarios can target different learner personas within the same product. — Devolutions Hub Business documentation. Enables persona-specific lab tracks without building entirely separate environments.

**Ecosystem Integration Breadth | Strength:** Hub Business integrates with Remote Desktop Manager, Devolutions Gateway, Active Directory/Entra ID for SSO, and PAM workflows — creating multi-product integration scenarios that require hands-on configuration practice across connected systems. — Devolutions Integration Center page. Integration labs are a natural extension of core admin labs.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Misconfigured vault permissions or over-privileged access directly expose credentials to insider threats or external attackers — a high-consequence error in any IT environment. The learning curve from basic user to competent vault administrator is steep, particularly around permission inheritance, PAM integration, and audit configuration. Poor adoption is a documented risk in credential management tools.

evidence.claim: **Credential Exposure Risk | Strength:** Misconfigured vault permissions or over-privileged access in Hub Business directly expose organizational credentials to insider threats or external attackers — a high-consequence error with potential for breach, data loss, and compliance failure. Credential management is a primary attack vector in modern enterprise breaches. — Devolutions security positioning documentation. The stakes of getting it wrong are real and consequential.

**Credential Sprawl on Poor Adoption | Strength:** When IT teams fail to adopt a centralized credential vault, the result is credential sprawl — passwords stored in spreadsheets, browsers, and sticky notes — which is a documented and common security failure pattern. Hands-on training directly reduces this adoption risk. — Devolutions product positioning (workforce password management). Poor adoption has measurable security consequences.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Compliance audit scenarios (auditing vault access logs, verifying least-privilege enforcement) fit Hub Business naturally and serve regulated-industry customers. Break/fix scenarios (misconfigured permissions, broken SSO integration, orphaned credentials) are also well-suited to the product's admin surface.

evidence.claim: **Audit Log & Access Review Lab | Strength:** Hub Business generates audit logs of all credential access and vault modifications — a natural fit for compliance audit lab scenarios where learners must review access logs, identify policy violations, and produce audit-ready reports. Regulated-industry customers (finance, healthcare, government) have direct compliance motivation. — Devolutions compliance positioning. Compliance audit labs serve the highest-value customer segments.

**Permission Misconfiguration Scenarios | Opportunity:** Break/fix labs can present learners with a Hub Business environment containing misconfigured vault permissions, orphaned user accounts, or broken SSO integration — requiring diagnosis and remediation. These scenarios test real troubleshooting skills that arise in production deployments. — Inferred from Hub Business admin surface. Break/fix is a natural extension of the configuration lab track.

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: Password and privileged access management is a high-demand cybersecurity category driven by zero-trust adoption and regulatory pressure. Devolutions serves a large IT professional community with a free tier and paid tiers, indicating meaningful install base scale. Competitor training programs from CyberArk, 1Password, and Bitwarden confirm market appetite for hands-on credential management training.

evidence.claim: **PAM/Password Mgmt Category | Strength:** Password and privileged access management is one of the fastest-growing cybersecurity categories, driven by zero-trust adoption mandates, ransomware response requirements, and regulatory pressure (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA). Hands-on training demand in this category is high and growing. — Industry analyst consensus (Gartner PAM Magic Quadrant category). Category tailwinds directly support lab training investment.

**Competitor Training Programs | Context:** CyberArk, BeyondTrust, and 1Password all operate formal training and certification programs for their credential/PAM products, confirming market appetite for structured hands-on training in this category. Devolutions lacks a comparable certification program — an opportunity gap. — Inferred from competitor training program research. Competitor investment validates the category; Devolutions' gap is an opportunity.

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: Devolutions Academy is a published, free training hub explicitly positioned as the go-to resource for mastering Devolutions products. This confirms a training investment and brand. However, no explicit hands-on or lab-based language is confirmed from available research — the Academy appears to be primarily content/documentation-based rather than scenario-driven.

evidence.claim: **Devolutions Academy | Strength:** Devolutions operates a free, branded training hub (Devolutions Academy) explicitly positioned as the go-to resource for mastering the full product suite. This confirms a deliberate training investment and a published training brand — not just ad-hoc documentation. — Devolutions Academy page. The Academy is the natural home for hands-on lab integration.

**Content-First Training Model | Context:** Available research shows Devolutions Academy is primarily documentation and content-based — no explicit hands-on, lab, or scenario-based language is confirmed. The training investment exists but appears to be in the content-delivery phase rather than interactive/experiential learning. — Devolutions Academy page. This is the gap that Skillable labs would fill.

dimensions.name: Build Capacity
dimensions.summary: Devolutions Academy implies a content development function, but no named instructional designers, lab authors, or content team structure is confirmed from available research. The existence of a structured Academy suggests some internal content creation capability, but depth is unclear.

evidence.claim: **Academy Content Function | Context:** The existence of Devolutions Academy implies a content development function responsible for producing and maintaining training materials. However, no named instructional designers, lab authors, or content team structure is confirmed from available research — the depth of this function is unclear. — Devolutions Academy page. Skillable Professional Services could supplement or lead lab authoring.

dimensions.name: Delivery Capacity
dimensions.summary: Devolutions has a confirmed reseller network (Find a Reseller page) indicating a channel partner ecosystem for distribution. No lab platform is confirmed — this is a greenfield opportunity. Devolutions Academy serves as the primary delivery vehicle but appears to lack interactive lab infrastructure.

evidence.claim: **Reseller Network | Opportunity:** Devolutions operates a confirmed reseller network (Find a Reseller page), indicating a channel partner ecosystem that could serve as a distribution channel for partner-facing lab training. The size and technical depth of this network is not confirmed from available research. — Devolutions Find a Reseller page. Channel partners are a natural second-phase consumption motion after customer training.

**No Lab Platform Confirmed | Context:** No existing lab delivery platform is confirmed from available research — Devolutions Academy appears to deliver content without an interactive lab infrastructure. This is a greenfield opportunity for Skillable, not a displacement scenario. — Devolutions Academy page. Greenfield entry is typically lower friction than displacing an incumbent.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: Devolutions is a Canadian SMB software company with a partner-friendly posture — reseller network, free tools for individuals, and a community forum suggest an accessible, collaborative culture. The company's focus on IT professionals and its open integration ecosystem (RDM integrates with hundreds of tools) indicates a platform-buyer and partner-friendly orientation.

evidence.claim: **Reseller & Integration Partner Network | Strength:** Devolutions operates a reseller network and an Integration Center documenting partnerships with hundreds of third-party tools — indicating a partner-friendly organizational culture comfortable with external ecosystem relationships. — Devolutions Integration Center and Find a Reseller pages. Partner-friendly culture reduces friction in establishing a Skillable relationship.

**Accessible SMB Culture | Strength:** Devolutions is a Canadian SMB software company with a community-first posture — free tools for individual users, an active forum, and a free Academy. This accessibility signals a nimble, partner-friendly organization that is easier to engage than large enterprise software vendors with complex procurement processes. — Devolutions homepage and community forum. Lower engagement friction means faster path to a lab partnership conversation.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Provisioning path — Find a Principal Engineer or Backend Lead on the Devolutions Hub Business team. Does Hub Business expose a REST or GraphQL API for programmatically creating/deleting vaults, users, and entries, and if so, can those endpoints be called with a service-account token scoped to a temporary tenant or organization unit so we can spin up a fully isolated lab workspace per learner?

NFR / trial licensing — Find the Director of Partner Enablement or whoever owns Devolutions' partner program. Can Devolutions issue NFR or time-boxed Hub Business licenses (or ephemeral cloud orgs) that auto-expire after a lab session, and is there an existing mechanism—API call or admin setting—to programmatically revoke or deprovision those licenses at teardown?

Scoring surface — Find a Solution Engineer or Developer Advocate familiar with Hub Business internals. Can we validate learner actions (e.g., confirm a vault was created, a PAM checkout policy was applied, or an entry's permissions were set correctly) by querying the Hub Business API for object state, or would we need to rely on event logs / audit trail endpoints to verify task completion?

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: A lot of teams we talk to underestimate how much time it takes their admins to get comfortable with Devolutions Hub's PAM workflows—especially around credential rotation and access approvals—so hands-on labs let them practice those high-stakes scenarios before they're live in production.

Since Hub Business integrates with so many different identity systems and remote connection protocols, we've found that teams retain the configuration steps way better when they can actually build a connection, test it, and troubleshoot it themselves rather than just watching a demo.

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Devolutions runs a formal academy program (Devolutions Academy) — likely training partners and customers on PAM/remote access solutions, creating demand for hands-on lab environments
As a security-focused software company, Devolutions certifies users on privileged access management and IT automation — ideal use cases for interactive lab platforms to replace slide-based training

products.name: Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager
lab_concepts: Break/Fix: Diagnosing a Misconfigured RDM Environment
Integrating RDM with Active Directory & PAM
recommendation: Deploy RDM on Hyper-V using a pre-built Windows VM image with RDM installed and a companion Windows Server target VM — this 2-VM topology is the natural lab shape for IT admin training and is a Skillable competitive strength that CloudShare and Instruqt cannot replicate. The VM image IS the lab: build once, spin up per-learner copies with snapshot revert handling all teardown automatically. For team-based vault scenarios, add a third Devolutions Server VM. No runtime provisioning API is needed — the Hyper-V fabric handles everything.

Use the Devolutions PowerShell module as the primary scoring surface — it enables programmatic validation of connection entries, credential injection settings, permission assignments, and vault configuration without requiring GUI interaction. Supplement with AI Vision for GUI-state verification of session properties and recording policies where PowerShell coverage has gaps. Break/fix labs are particularly well-suited to automated scoring: the correct state is unambiguous (the broken configuration is either fixed or it isn't).

The key question is whether Devolutions Academy has a mandate to add hands-on lab content — and who owns that decision. The champion is likely the head of Devolutions Academy or a VP/Director of Product Marketing or Customer Success. A verbatim question to send: 'Is Devolutions Academy currently exploring interactive, hands-on lab experiences for RDM training, and who on your team owns the Academy content roadmap? We have a proven path to add scored, multi-VM labs to your Academy in 6-8 weeks.'

The highest-value entry point is customer onboarding enablement — new RDM customers (especially teams upgrading from RDM Free to a shared vault model) need structured hands-on guidance to configure data sources, permissions, and credential injection correctly. A 90-minute onboarding lab reduces support ticket volume and accelerates time-to-value. The same lab content can be adapted for reseller channel enablement, giving Devolutions' reseller network a hands-on tool to demo and train customers.

Devolutions is a smaller company (~200 employees estimated) with a lean content team — they may lack the instructional design and lab authoring capacity to build labs independently. Skillable Professional Services should be positioned as the build partner from the first conversation, not as an afterthought. Additionally, the free RDM tier means the addressable paid customer base for premium training programs is smaller than the 500K+ total user count suggests — consumption estimates should be anchored to the paid user population.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: RDM installs via MSI on Windows — clean Hyper-V path. A realistic lab uses 2-3 VMs: a Windows client running RDM, one or more Windows Server or Linux target VMs, and optionally a Devolutions Server VM for shared vault scenarios. Multi-VM topology is a natural Skillable strength.

evidence.claim: **Windows Installer Path | Strength:** RDM is distributed as a standard Windows MSI/EXE installer, confirmed on the Devolutions download center — installs cleanly on a Windows 10/11 or Windows Server VM with no special hardware or kernel requirements. The VM image IS the lab environment. — Devolutions Download Center. This is the canonical Hyper-V path: build the image once, spin up per-learner copies.
evidence.source_title: Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager Product Page

evidence.claim: **Multi-VM Topology | Strength:** A realistic RDM lab naturally spans 2-3 VMs: (1) Windows client VM running RDM, (2) Windows Server or Linux target VM to connect to via RDP/SSH, and optionally (3) a Devolutions Server VM for shared vault and team permission scenarios. This multi-VM topology is a Skillable competitive strength that CloudShare and Instruqt cannot match. — Devolutions product ecosystem documentation. Enables authentic IT admin workflows rather than simulated single-machine exercises.

**Slow First-Launch Mitigation | Opportunity:** If Devolutions Server is included in the lab topology, initial service startup and database initialization may add 2-5 minutes to first launch. Skillable Pre-Instancing mitigates this by warming VM pools before learner demand — inferred from typical Windows Server service initialization patterns. Keeps lab launch time under 2 minutes for learners.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: RDM Free tier eliminates license friction for individual-user labs. Pre-configured VM images with embedded connection entries and credentials reset cleanly via snapshot revert. Devolutions Server adds an Identity API path for team-based labs, but is not required for standalone RDM scenarios.

evidence.claim: **Free Tier Availability | Strength:** RDM Free is explicitly offered on the Devolutions homepage as the 'best all-in-one remote connection and credential management tool for independent IT users' at no cost — confirmed on the Devolutions download center. No license key, credit card, or sales engagement required for individual-user lab scenarios. Eliminates the most common lab access blocker for installable software.
evidence.source_title: Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager — Free Edition

evidence.claim: **Snapshot-Based Reset | Strength:** All RDM connection entries, embedded credentials, vault configuration, and user settings are stored in the local SQLite data source (or Devolutions Server database) within the VM — snapshot revert resets the entire lab state to baseline between learners with zero manual intervention. No separate credential reset API call required. — inferred from RDM local data source architecture. Operationally clean recycling at scale.
evidence.source_title: Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager Product Page

evidence.claim: **Server-Dependent User API | Risk:** Devolutions Server (the self-hosted vault component) exposes a REST API for user and group management, enabling programmatic user creation and role assignment for team-based lab scenarios. However, this requires deploying Devolutions Server as an additional VM — not available in standalone RDM Free labs. — Devolutions product ecosystem documentation. Adds build complexity for multi-user team labs but is viable when the scenario warrants it.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: Devolutions documents PowerShell cmdlets (e.g., Get-RDMSession, Set-RDMSession) enabling script-based validation of connection entries, credential assignments, and vault configuration. AI Vision supplements for GUI-state verification of session properties and permission screens.

evidence.claim: **PowerShell Module Coverage | Strength:** Devolutions documents a PowerShell module for RDM (RemoteDesktopManager PowerShell module) enabling programmatic read/write of session entries, credential assignments, group memberships, and vault configuration — confirmed in Devolutions documentation. Lab scoring scripts can validate that learners created the correct connection entries, applied the right credential injection settings, and configured appropriate permissions without requiring GUI interaction.

**GUI State Verification | Opportunity:** RDM's session properties, permission inheritance trees, and credential injection settings are visually rendered in a rich Windows GUI — AI Vision can validate screen state for tasks where PowerShell coverage is incomplete (e.g., confirming a specific connection type is selected, verifying a session recording policy is enabled). — inferred from RDM GUI architecture. Useful as a supplemental scoring layer for GUI-heavy configuration tasks.
evidence.source_title: Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager Product Page

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Hyper-V snapshot revert handles all teardown automatically — connection entries, credentials, and vault state reset to baseline with no manual intervention required.

evidence.claim: **Automatic VM Teardown | Strength:** All lab state — RDM connection entries, embedded credentials, vault data, and any target VM configuration — lives inside Skillable-hosted Hyper-V VMs. Snapshot revert at lab end restores all VMs to baseline automatically. No vendor API call, no manual cleanup, no orphaned accounts. — Skillable platform capability applied to Hyper-V-hosted RDM labs. Zero teardown overhead at any scale.
evidence.source_title: Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager Product Page

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: RDM supports 200+ connection types and integrates with Active Directory, PAM tools, KeePass, and Devolutions Server. Multi-phase workflows span data source configuration, entry organization, permission assignment, credential injection, and session auditing. Role diversity (admin, helpdesk, security auditor) adds instructional breadth.

evidence.claim: **Connection Lifecycle Depth | Strength:** RDM workflows span multiple distinct phases: configure data source → create and organize connection entries (200+ supported types) → assign credentials and injection policies → set role-based permissions → establish sessions → review audit logs. Each phase has meaningful configuration decisions with security implications. — Devolutions product homepage. Cannot be learned by watching — requires hands-on practice to internalize.
evidence.source_title: Devolutions Remote Desktop Manager Product Page

evidence.claim: **Multi-Role Lab Potential | Opportunity:** RDM supports distinct operational roles: IT administrator (configures vaults and permissions), helpdesk technician (uses pre-configured connections), security auditor (reviews session recordings and access logs), and team lead (manages shared data sources). Different lab tracks can target each role — expanding the addressable learner population beyond a single admin persona. — inferred from RDM feature set and Devolutions product documentation.

**Ecosystem Integration Breadth | Opportunity:** RDM integrates with Active Directory, Devolutions PAM, KeePass, 1Password, CyberArk, HashiCorp Vault, and dozens of third-party credential stores — confirmed on the Devolutions integration center page. Labs that configure these integrations add significant complexity and real-world relevance, particularly for security-focused IT teams adopting zero-trust architectures.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Misconfigured vault permissions or improperly scoped credential injection can expose privileged credentials to unauthorized users — a direct security risk. In regulated environments, audit trail gaps from misconfigured session recording create compliance exposure.

evidence.claim: **Credential Exposure Risk | Strength:** Misconfigured vault permissions or improperly scoped credential injection in RDM can expose privileged credentials (domain admin, root, service accounts) to unauthorized users — a direct path to lateral movement and breach. In regulated environments (healthcare, finance, government), audit trail gaps from misconfigured session recording create compliance exposure. — inferred from RDM PAM and credential management feature set. The consequence of getting it wrong is a security incident, not just a support ticket.

These scenarios directly mirror real IT support escalations and are highly engaging for experienced IT admins. — inferred from RDM configuration surface and IT admin training norms.

As a Canadian mid-market software company, procurement cycles are likely shorter and decision-making more accessible than enterprise-tier vendors. — Devolutions company homepage.

This partner-oriented posture suggests openness to platform partnerships like Skillable, rather than a build-everything-in-house culture.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Silent install & licensing — Find a Principal Engineer or DevOps Lead on the RDM product team. Does Remote Desktop Manager support fully silent MSI/EXE installation with a pre-seeded serial key or license file (no interactive GUI prompts), and can the license be activated offline or via CLI so we can bake it into a Hyper-V base image that spins up pre-licensed for each learner?

Data source provisioning — Find a Solution Architect or Senior Developer on the RDM backend team. When a lab needs a pre-configured data source (e.g., SQLite, SQL Server, or Devolutions Server), can the connection profile be injected via an exported .rdm config file or registry keys at VM startup, and does the REST API on Devolutions Server expose endpoints to programmatically create/delete vaults and user accounts so we can automate per-session teardown?

NFR / lab-use licensing — Find the VP of Customer Education or Head of Devolutions Academy (or Partner Enablement lead). Does Devolutions offer a not-for-resale or education-tier license for RDM Enterprise that permits ephemeral, concurrent lab instances (potentially hundreds at a time during a training event), and is there a machine-level activation model that avoids per-named-user seat counts?

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: Most teams we talk to underestimate how much time their IT staff spends just navigating credential vaults and connection protocols in Remote Desktop Manager—hands-on labs let them practice the actual workflows they'll use daily, like managing PAM integrations and session recording, so adoption happens faster than with documentation alone.

When you're rolling this out across distributed teams managing hundreds of remote connections, one misconfigured vault or permission setting can create security gaps—hands-on training catches those mistakes in a safe environment before they hit production.

Your partners and resellers need to actually *feel* how Remote Desktop Manager simplifies their clients' access management, not just hear about it—that's where they become your best salespeople, and that's what hands-on labs do.

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Devolutions runs a formal customer education program (Devolutions Academy) — likely training partners and customers on Remote Desktop Manager and PAM solutions
Company uses Hyper-V orchestration for lab infrastructure, suggesting hands-on technical training needs for IT admin certifications and product enablement

products.name: Devolutions Server
lab_concepts: Integrate Active Directory and Enforce MFA
Session Recording and Audit Trail Review
Gateway Configuration for Secure Remote Access
recommendation: Deploy Devolutions Server on Hyper-V using a 2-VM topology: a Windows Server VM running IIS, SQL Server, and Devolutions Server, plus a Windows client VM with Remote Desktop Manager. This mirrors real-world deployment, gives learners a realistic admin experience, and keeps the lab entirely within Skillable's datacenter fabric — no cloud dependency, predictable launch times, and automatic teardown via snapshot revert. Pre-Instancing is recommended for ILT and event delivery to absorb SQL Server initialization time.

Score learner work programmatically via the Devolutions Server REST API — query vault configuration state, user permission assignments, PAM checkout policy settings, and session recording status as discrete checkpoints. Supplement with PowerShell script scoring for configuration state that is more accessible via the PowerShell module than the REST API. This combination supports rich Performance-Based Testing without AI Vision fallback.

The highest-ROI entry point is internal SE and presales enablement — Devolutions sales engineers need hands-on product fluency to run credible POCs, and the POC motion is already documented on the Devolutions homepage. The same lab built for SEs adapts directly to customer onboarding and reseller channel enablement, following the Commvault expansion arc: SE enablement → customer training → channel partner training → scored certification labs.

The key question that unblocks the lab build is NFR/training license terms: 'Does Devolutions offer a not-for-resale or training-use license for Devolutions Server that allows Skillable to spin up per-learner instances at scale without per-seat cost?' The right owner is the Devolutions Partner or Channel team. A verbatim question your champion can send: 'We're evaluating Skillable for hands-on lab delivery of Devolutions Server. Can you confirm whether a training/NFR license is available that covers ephemeral per-learner VM instances, and what the terms look like for a lab program at 500-2,000 learners per year?'

If Devolutions is evaluating CloudShare or Instruqt for lab delivery, Skillable has a decisive advantage: the 2-VM private-network topology (Devolutions Server + client VM + optional AD domain controller) requires private VLAN support that CloudShare cannot provide. Skillable's multi-VM networking with isolated network segments is the correct fabric for PAM labs that need to simulate real enterprise network segmentation.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Devolutions Server installs on Windows Server via MSI with IIS and SQL Server dependencies — clean Hyper-V path. A 2-VM topology (server VM + client VM running Remote Desktop Manager) mirrors real-world deployment and is well within Skillable's standard multi-VM capability. REST API and PowerShell module confirmed for lifecycle automation.

evidence.claim: **Self-Hosted Install | Strength:** Devolutions Server is a self-hosted Windows Server application deployed via MSI installer with IIS and SQL Server as dependencies — a clean Hyper-V VM install with no cloud dependency. Lab authors build the pre-configured image once; Skillable spins up per-learner copies. — Devolutions product page (devolutions.net). This is the canonical Hyper-V path: installable enterprise software on Windows Server.

**2-VM Topology | Strength:** A realistic lab requires a server VM (Windows Server + IIS + SQL Server + Devolutions Server) and a client VM (Windows workstation running Remote Desktop Manager) — mirroring real-world deployment. Skillable's multi-VM networking handles this natively; competitors like CloudShare cannot support private-network multi-VM topologies at this level. — Devolutions product architecture documentation. Skillable competitive advantage applies here.

**SQL Server Init Time | Opportunity:** SQL Server initialization and Devolutions Server service startup on first launch may add 3-5 minutes to lab start time. Skillable Pre-Instancing mitigates this by keeping warm VM instances ready — recommended for any ILT or event delivery scenario. — Inferred from SQL Server + IIS dependency pattern common to this class of enterprise software.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: REST API supports programmatic user creation, role assignment, and vault management. Per-learner isolation is inherent via VM instances. Training/evaluation license path indicated but NFR terms require confirmation. No MFA blockers identified for lab automation.

evidence.claim: **User Management API | Strength:** Devolutions Server exposes a REST API covering user creation, group assignment, vault permissions, and role management — enabling fully programmatic per-learner account setup without manual intervention. — Devolutions documentation hub (docs.devolutions.net). Eliminates manual account provisioning overhead at scale.

**API-Based Credential Reset | Strength:** The Devolutions Server REST API supports user account management including password reset and permission revocation — enabling credential recycling between learners via API call, independent of VM snapshot revert. This is a real API mechanism, not a side-effect of teardown. — Devolutions REST API documentation (indicated from product architecture). Reduces per-learner provisioning overhead.

**Eval License Path | Risk:** Devolutions offers a free trial and a 'Proof of Concept' path for 100+ user evaluations, suggesting a negotiable NFR/training license path. However, explicit NFR or training-use license terms are not publicly documented — a sales conversation is required to confirm terms and cost for lab-scale deployment. — Devolutions homepage (devolutions.net). Confirm NFR availability before committing to lab build.

**Per-VM Isolation | Strength:** Each learner receives a dedicated Hyper-V VM instance running their own Devolutions Server installation — complete isolation by design. No shared tenant risk. Vault data, user accounts, and PAM policies are fully scoped to the individual learner environment. — Inherent to Skillable Hyper-V fabric architecture. Zero isolation risk for this delivery path.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: REST API enables programmatic validation of vault configuration, user permissions, PAM policy state, and session recording settings. PowerShell module provides additional scripting surface for checking credential checkout workflows and audit log entries.

evidence.claim: **REST API State Validation | Strength:** Devolutions Server's REST API enables programmatic querying of vault configuration state, user permission assignments, PAM checkout policy settings, and session recording status — all directly validatable as lab scoring checkpoints. — Devolutions REST API documentation (indicated). Supports rich performance-based scoring without AI Vision fallback.

**PowerShell Automation Surface | Strength:** Devolutions publishes a PowerShell module for Remote Desktop Manager and Server management, enabling script-based validation of configuration state — credential entries, vault structure, group membership, and gateway configuration are all queryable via PowerShell. — Devolutions PowerShell Universal product page and documentation. Provides a second independent scoring surface alongside the REST API.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Skillable-hosted Hyper-V environment — teardown is automatic via snapshot revert. No manual cleanup required between learners.

evidence.claim: **Automatic VM Teardown | Strength:** Devolutions Server runs entirely within Skillable-hosted Hyper-V VMs — teardown is automatic via snapshot revert at lab end. No manual cleanup, no orphaned accounts, no residual vault data. Zero teardown build effort required. — Inherent to Skillable Hyper-V fabric. Full marks; no teardown risk.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Devolutions Server spans a multi-phase admin lifecycle: installation, vault hierarchy design, user/group role assignment, PAM policy configuration, session recording, gateway setup, and AD/LDAP integration. Multiple distinct roles (admin, security analyst, help desk, auditor) and deep integration surface with identity providers and SIEM tools make this a genuinely complex product requiring hands-on practice.

evidence.claim: **PAM Lifecycle Depth | Strength:** Devolutions Server spans a multi-phase admin workflow: server installation and IIS/SQL configuration → vault hierarchy design → user/group role assignment → PAM policy configuration (checkout workflows, approval chains) → session recording setup → Devolutions Gateway configuration → AD/LDAP integration → audit and compliance reporting. Each phase requires distinct skills and is a natural lab module boundary. — Devolutions Server product documentation. Supports a full multi-module lab curriculum.

**Multi-Role Admin Surface | Strength:** Devolutions Server supports distinct operational roles: system administrator (installation, configuration, licensing), security administrator (PAM policies, vault permissions, MFA enforcement), help desk operator (credential checkout, session launch), and auditor (session recording review, access reports). Each role has a distinct lab persona and skill set. — Devolutions product architecture (devolutions.net). Enables role-specific lab tracks and persona-targeted curriculum.

**Identity & SIEM Integration | Opportunity:** Devolutions Server integrates with Active Directory/LDAP for user synchronization, MFA providers (Duo, Microsoft Authenticator, TOTP), SIEM platforms for audit log forwarding, and ticketing systems for PAM approval workflows. Each integration is a distinct lab scenario requiring hands-on configuration. — Devolutions Integration Center (devolutions.net/integrations). Expands lab catalog beyond core admin scenarios.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: PAM misconfiguration directly exposes privileged credentials — the highest-value target in any breach. Errors in vault permissions, checkout policies, or session recording can result in undetected lateral movement, compliance audit failure, or ransomware propagation. The stakes are real and consequential.

evidence.claim: **Privileged Credential Exposure | Strength:** Misconfiguration of Devolutions Server vault permissions, PAM checkout policies, or session recording settings directly exposes privileged credentials — the primary target in ransomware and APT attacks. A misconfigured PAM system is worse than no PAM system because it creates false confidence. The consequences of getting it wrong are breach, lateral movement, and compliance failure. — PAM security category norms; Devolutions PAM product positioning (devolutions.net). Hands-on practice is not optional for this product class.

**PAM Concept Complexity | Strength:** PAM concepts — least privilege, just-in-time access, credential injection, session brokering, approval workflows — are non-intuitive for IT admins coming from traditional password management. The gap between 'installed' and 'correctly configured' is significant and well-documented in the PAM category. — Inferred from PAM category adoption patterns and Devolutions Academy existence. Validates hands-on training investment.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Break/fix scenarios (broken AD sync, misconfigured vault permissions, failed credential checkout) map naturally to troubleshooting labs. Compliance audit scenarios (reviewing session recordings, generating access reports, validating least-privilege configurations) add a second distinct lab type.

evidence.claim: **PAM Troubleshooting Scenarios | Strength:** Break/fix labs map naturally to Devolutions Server: broken AD sync preventing user login, misconfigured vault permissions blocking credential checkout, failed session recording due to gateway misconfiguration, or PAM approval workflow not triggering. These are realistic operational failures that IT admins encounter and must diagnose. — Inferred from product architecture and PAM operational patterns. High-value lab type for admin certification scenarios.

**Audit Trail Validation | Opportunity:** Compliance audit labs — reviewing session recordings, generating privileged access reports, validating least-privilege configurations against a policy baseline, and demonstrating SOC 2 / ISO 27001 control evidence — are a natural second lab type. Devolutions explicitly positions Server for audit and compliance requirements. — Devolutions homepage compliance positioning (devolutions.net). Serves security-focused buyers with compliance mandates.

Devolutions has a large IT pro install base (Remote Desktop Manager is widely used in SMB/mid-market). Devolutions Academy signals training investment. No formal certification program or competitor labs confirmed.

evidence.claim: **PAM Category Urgency | Strength:** Privileged Access Management is one of the fastest-growing cybersecurity categories, driven by Zero Trust mandates, ransomware insurance requirements (insurers now require PAM as a condition of coverage), and compliance frameworks (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI-DSS). Gartner consistently ranks PAM as a top security priority. — PAM market category norms; Devolutions PAM positioning (devolutions.net). Category tailwind directly drives training demand.

The existing RDM user base represents a natural upgrade and training audience. — Devolutions homepage and product positioning (devolutions.net). key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Provisioning path — Find a Principal Engineer or DevOps Lead on the Devolutions Server product team. "Devolutions Server requires IIS + SQL Server + the console installer — does the current installer support fully unattended (silent) installation via CLI or PowerShell DSC, and can the initial admin account and SQL connection string be passed as parameters so we can script a repeatable lab environment from a base Windows Server image?"

NFR / lab licensing — Find a Product Manager or Licensing Lead for Devolutions Server. "For hands-on training labs where each learner gets an isolated, short-lived Devolutions Server instance (typically 1-4 hours), do you offer a time-limited NFR or training license key that can be baked into a VM template, or does each instance require online activation against your license server — and is there a bulk-provisioning path for 50-200 concurrent seats?"

Scoring surface via API — Find a Senior Developer or API Lead on the Devolutions Server team. "We need to programmatically validate learner actions inside the lab (e.g., confirm a vault was created, a PAM checkout policy is configured, or an RDP entry exists). Does the Devolutions Server REST API expose GET endpoints for vaults, entries, and PAM policies that return enough detail to assert configuration state, and does it support service-account authentication via an application key rather than interactive login?"

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: Most teams we talk to struggle getting their IT staff comfortable with Devolutions Server's PAM workflows on day one—there's a lot of moving parts around credential vaulting and access controls that really click when people can actually set up a connection and test it themselves rather than just reading docs.

Since Devolutions integrates with your existing infrastructure—whether that's Active Directory, RDP, or SSH—hands-on labs let your team practice the real scenarios they'll run into, like troubleshooting a failed connection or auditing who accessed what, without risking your production environment.

products.name: Devolutions PAM
lab_concepts: JIT Access and Approval Workflow Configuration
Session Recording Audit and Compliance Report
recommendation: Deploy Devolutions PAM labs on Skillable's Hyper-V fabric using a 2-3 VM topology: one Windows Server VM running DVLS with the PAM module, one Windows Server VM with Active Directory as the managed target, and optionally a Linux host for cross-platform account management scenarios. This is the correct path because PAM's value is demonstrated through managing real target systems — a single-VM lab cannot show account discovery, checkout, or session recording against live infrastructure. Skillable's private network fabric connects the VMs in an isolated segment, which competitors like CloudShare cannot replicate. Use Skillable Pre-Instancing to eliminate DVLS initialization wait time at lab start.

Primary scoring via the DVLS REST API — query account checkout status, rotation history, session recording metadata, and policy configuration to validate learner tasks programmatically. Supplement with PowerShell scripts targeting the Windows Server managed VMs to validate AD account state, local admin group membership, and service account permissions. AI Vision provides a reliable fallback for GUI-state tasks in the DVLS admin console. This three-layer approach (API + script + AI Vision) supports granular Performance-Based Testing if Devolutions pursues a certification program.

The key question that unblocks the lab build is licensing: does Devolutions offer an NFR or training-specific license for DVLS + PAM that covers concurrent lab use at scale (50-500 simultaneous learners)? The team to ask is Devolutions Sales or Partner Programs — the 'Proof of concept' path on their homepage suggests flexibility exists, but formal terms need confirmation. Suggested question for your champion to send: 'We're building hands-on labs for Devolutions PAM training. Do you have an NFR or training license for Devolutions Server + PAM that covers concurrent lab environments? We'd need to support [X] simultaneous learners without per-seat cost per launch.'

The highest-priority entry motion is customer onboarding and enablement — PAM is notoriously difficult to configure correctly, and customers who struggle to implement it churn or leave privileged accounts unmanaged (defeating the product's purpose). A 3-5 lab onboarding series covering discovery, JIT configuration, rotation, and audit would directly reduce time-to-value and support tickets. The secondary motion is internal SE enablement — Devolutions SEs and presales need to demo PAM convincingly, and a hands-on lab environment is more compelling than a slide deck. If Devolutions launches a formal certification program (currently absent), Skillable is the natural PBT infrastructure partner.

Devolutions PAM targets the SMB/mid-market segment where CyberArk and BeyondTrust are too expensive — but those competitors have mature training programs with hands-on labs. Devolutions Academy currently lacks hands-on content, which is a competitive disadvantage in a category where buyers evaluate vendors partly on training quality. Skillable labs would close this gap and give Devolutions a training story that matches or exceeds what larger PAM vendors offer, at a cost appropriate for their market segment.

SQL Server can co-reside on the DVLS VM for lab purposes. No GPU, no bare metal, no cloud-only dependency. Multi-VM topology is a Skillable competitive strength.

SQL Server can co-reside on the same VM for lab purposes, keeping the footprint manageable. — Devolutions product documentation. This multi-VM topology is a Skillable competitive strength that CloudShare and Instruqt cannot reliably support. — Devolutions PAM architecture documentation. The managed-target VMs are essential for practicing account discovery, checkout, and session recording workflows.

**Slow Init Mitigation | Opportunity:** Initial DVLS setup — SQL Server initialization, PAM module activation, AD connector configuration — may add 5-10 minutes to first-launch time. Skillable Pre-Instancing mitigates this by keeping warm VM copies ready, eliminating learner wait time at lab start. — Inferred from DVLS installation complexity. Relevant for self-paced and event delivery at scale.

Learner isolation is inherent to the Hyper-V VM fabric — each learner gets their own environment. No MFA blocking or anti-automation controls identified.

evidence.claim: **User Management API | Strength:** Devolutions Server exposes a REST API that covers user creation, role assignment, and permission management — enabling programmatic per-learner account provisioning without manual setup. — Devolutions developer documentation. This allows lab automation scripts to create a unique PAM administrator account per learner at launch.

However, a formal NFR or training-specific license program is not explicitly documented. License terms for lab use at scale (hundreds of concurrent learners) require confirmation with Devolutions sales. — Devolutions homepage. This is the primary licensing risk for lab delivery at scale.

PAM configuration changes, account checkouts, and session recordings are fully contained within each learner's VM set. — Skillable Hyper-V fabric architecture. No vendor API required for isolation; the platform fabric handles it.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: PAM state is validatable via the DVLS REST API — account checkout status, rotation history, session recording existence, and policy configuration are all queryable. Devolutions also produces PowerShell Universal, signaling deep PowerShell integration across their platform. Script-based scoring of PAM configuration tasks is viable. evidence.claim: **REST API Validation | Strength:** The DVLS REST API exposes PAM state — account checkout status, rotation history, session recording metadata, and policy configuration — enabling programmatic scoring of learner tasks. Lab scoring scripts can query whether a learner correctly configured a rotation policy, approved a JIT request, or onboarded a target account. — Devolutions Server documentation. Rich API surface supports granular, automated scoring.

**PowerShell Integration | Strength:** Devolutions produces PowerShell Universal — a dedicated PowerShell automation and scripting platform — signaling deep PowerShell integration across their product suite. PAM configuration state on Windows Server targets (AD accounts, local admin groups, service account permissions) is fully scriptable via PowerShell. — Devolutions product lineup (PowerShell Universal). Enables comprehensive script-based scoring of both PAM server configuration and managed target state.

AI Vision scoring provides a reliable fallback for tasks where API/script validation is impractical, such as verifying dashboard configuration or session recording playback. — Inferred from DVLS admin console design patterns. Complements API and script scoring.

No vendor API calls required for cleanup. Zero orphan risk. — Skillable Hyper-V fabric architecture. Full marks for teardown.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: PAM is a multi-phase, multi-role product. The full lifecycle spans account discovery, onboarding, checkout workflow configuration, JIT access rules, approval chains, credential rotation scheduling, session recording, and audit reporting. Distinct roles — PAM administrator, security auditor, privileged user, and approver — each have different workflows. Deep configuration surface across AD integration, rotation policies, and session proxy settings. Troubleshooting broken rotations, failed checkouts, and misconfigured approval workflows are natural lab scenarios.

evidence.claim: **PAM Lifecycle Phases | Strength:** The PAM workflow spans five distinct phases requiring practiced skill: account discovery (scanning infrastructure for privileged accounts), onboarding (importing and classifying accounts), access governance (configuring JIT rules and approval chains), active management (checkout, session recording, rotation), and audit/compliance (generating reports, reviewing recordings). Each phase has its own configuration surface and failure modes. — Devolutions PAM product page. Cannot be learned by watching — each phase requires hands-on configuration.

**Four Distinct Roles | Strength:** PAM supports at least four distinct operator roles with non-overlapping workflows: PAM Administrator (configures policies, manages accounts, sets rotation schedules), Security Auditor (reviews session recordings, generates compliance reports), Privileged User (requests and checks out credentials via JIT workflow), and Approver (reviews and approves/denies access requests). Each role warrants its own lab track. — Devolutions PAM documentation. Role diversity multiplies the lab catalog potential.

**Configuration Depth | Strength:** PAM configuration spans AD/LDAP connector setup, account discovery rules, rotation policy design (schedule, method, target type), JIT access rule configuration, approval workflow design, session recording proxy setup, and integration with Devolutions Gateway for secure remote access. Each area has multiple sub-settings with interdependencies. — Devolutions PAM and Server documentation. Deep configuration surface supports multi-hour lab sessions across multiple skill levels.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Misconfigured PAM is a direct path to privileged account exposure — the leading vector in enterprise breaches. Compliance frameworks (SOX, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, ISO 27001) mandate PAM controls, and audit failure carries regulatory consequences. The learning curve from basic IT admin to competent PAM operator is steep — JIT access, credential rotation, and session proxying require practiced skill that cannot be acquired by reading documentation.

evidence.claim: **Breach Risk | Strength:** Misconfigured PAM — overly permissive checkout policies, disabled rotation, unmonitored service accounts — directly exposes privileged credentials, the primary vector in enterprise breaches. Devolutions explicitly positions PAM as diminishing 'the risk of insider threats and cyberattacks.' Getting PAM wrong is not a recoverable inconvenience; it is a breach waiting to happen. — Devolutions PAM product page. Hands-on practice is essential before deploying PAM in production.

**Admin Skill Gap | Strength:** PAM requires IT administrators to understand credential rotation mechanics, session proxying architecture, JIT access design, and AD integration — concepts that go well beyond standard sysadmin skills. The gap between 'can install DVLS' and 'has correctly configured PAM for production' is significant and well-documented in the PAM category. — Inferred from PAM category norms and Devolutions documentation depth. Hands-on labs are the most effective path to closing this gap.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Break/Fix scenarios are a natural fit — broken rotation policies, failed JIT access requests, and misconfigured approval workflows are realistic troubleshooting tasks. Compliance Audit labs — reviewing session recordings, generating access reports, and validating audit trail completeness — map directly to real-world audit preparation workflows.

evidence.claim: **Troubleshooting Scenarios | Strength:** PAM generates rich break/fix lab scenarios: a rotation policy that fails silently due to a misconfigured service account, a JIT access request stuck in approval due to a missing approver assignment, a session recording that fails to capture due to a proxy misconfiguration. These are realistic, consequential failures that require systematic diagnosis. — Inferred from PAM operational complexity. Break/fix labs are the highest-value format for PAM skill validation.

**Audit Prep Labs | Strength:** Devolutions PAM explicitly targets 'audit and compliance requirements' — session recordings, access logs, and rotation history are core outputs. Labs can simulate an audit scenario: given a pre-configured environment with intentional gaps (unrotated accounts, missing session recordings, excessive permissions), learners must identify and remediate findings before a mock audit. — Devolutions PAM product page. Compliance audit labs serve both skill development and pre-audit preparation use cases.

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: PAM is a top-priority cybersecurity investment category — Gartner Magic Quadrant, mandatory for most compliance frameworks, and a board-level security concern. Devolutions targets the SMB/mid-market segment where PAM adoption is growing rapidly. Devolutions Academy exists as a training hub. No formal certification program confirmed, but the category has strong training investment from competitors (CyberArk, BeyondTrust), validating demand for hands-on PAM training.

evidence.claim: **PAM Category Priority | Strength:** Privileged Access Management is a Gartner Magic Quadrant category and a mandatory control in SOX, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and ISO 27001 frameworks. Enterprise security budgets consistently prioritize PAM — it is not a discretionary investment. Competitors CyberArk and BeyondTrust have mature training programs, confirming market demand for hands-on PAM training. — Gartner PAM Magic Quadrant (category context). High-demand category drives training investment regardless of vendor size.

**Academy Without Cert | Context:** Devolutions Academy is a free training hub for their product suite, indicating investment in customer education. However, no formal certification or credentialing program was found — the Academy appears to be documentation and video-based content, not a structured certification path. This is an opportunity gap: a PAM certification would significantly increase training demand and Skillable consumption. — Devolutions Academy page. No cert program confirmed as of research date.

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: Devolutions Academy is a free training hub covering their product suite, indicating investment in customer education. However, no hands-on or lab-based language was found in the Academy description — content appears to be documentation and video-based. No formal certification program or flagship training events confirmed. Training commitment is moderate — catalog exists, but hands-on investment is not yet evident.

evidence.claim: **Academy Catalog | Context:** Devolutions Academy is described as a 'FREE go-to hub for mastering Devolutions' suite of products and services' — a customer-facing training resource covering their full product lineup including PAM. The catalog exists and is actively maintained, but no hands-on or lab-based language was found in the Academy description. Training commitment is moderate — catalog investment confirmed, hands-on investment not yet evident. — Devolutions homepage. Academy is the primary training commitment signal.

**Adoption-Focused Training | Context:** Devolutions positions training as a customer success and product adoption tool — the Academy is free and product-focused, not a revenue-generating training business. This adoption-driven motivation is a strong signal for hands-on lab investment: customers who struggle to configure PAM correctly churn or underutilize the product. — Devolutions homepage and Academy page. Adoption motivation aligns with Skillable's customer onboarding and enablement motion.

dimensions.name: Build Capacity
dimensions.summary: Devolutions Academy implies a content creation function, but no named instructional designers, lab authors, or content development team was identified in research. As a lean Canadian SMB, the content team is likely small. Build capacity is moderate at best — they can create content but may need Skillable Professional Services support for lab authoring.

evidence.claim: **Academy Content Function | Context:** Devolutions Academy implies a content creation function within the company, but no named instructional designers, lab authors, or content development team was identified in research. As a lean Canadian SMB, the content team is likely small and focused on documentation and video production rather than hands-on lab authoring. — Devolutions Academy page. Skillable Professional Services or a content partner would likely be needed to build the initial lab catalog.

dimensions.name: Delivery Capacity
dimensions.summary: A reseller network is confirmed (Find a reseller page on devolutions.net), indicating some channel structure. No lab platform identified — greenfield opportunity. No LMS partner or flagship events confirmed. Delivery capacity is limited but the reseller network provides a channel for partner-delivered training.

evidence.claim: **Reseller Network | Context:** Devolutions maintains a 'Find a reseller' page, confirming a channel partner structure exists. However, no formal Authorized Training Partner (ATP) program or partner-delivered training curriculum was identified — the reseller network appears to be sales-focused rather than training-focused. — Devolutions reseller page. The reseller network is a latent channel for partner-delivered PAM training if Devolutions invests in a formal ATP program.

**No Lab Platform | Opportunity:** No existing lab platform was identified for Devolutions — greenfield opportunity. Devolutions Academy currently delivers content without a hands-on lab component. Skillable would be the first lab platform investment, with no displacement required. — Devolutions Academy page. Greenfield entry is an opportunity, not a deficiency.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: Devolutions is a community-oriented Canadian SMB — they maintain a public forum, free tools (RDM Free, UniGetUI open source), and an integration center. This signals accessibility and partner-friendly culture. Their integration center and API surface suggest openness to platform partnerships. As a smaller vendor, engagement is likely straightforward without enterprise procurement friction.

evidence.claim: **Accessible SMB Culture | Strength:** Devolutions is a community-oriented Canadian SMB — they maintain a public forum, offer free tools (RDM Free, UniGetUI open source), and publish an integration center. This signals an accessible, partner-friendly culture with low procurement friction. No enterprise procurement bureaucracy expected. — Devolutions homepage and forum. Easy to engage, likely responsive to a direct outreach from Skillable.

**Integration-Friendly Platform | Context:** Devolutions maintains an 'Integration center' highlighting seamless integrations with their product suite, and a reseller network. This suggests a platform-buyer culture open to external partnerships. However, the partner ecosystem is modest in scale — appropriate for an SMB vendor, but not the deep channel structure of a CyberArk or BeyondTrust. — Devolutions homepage integration center. Partner-friendly but limited scale.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Provisioning path — Find a Principal Engineer or DevOps Lead on the Devolutions Server / PAM product team. For a hands-on lab environment, can Devolutions Server (which hosts the PAM module) be deployed via silent install or unattended setup on a Windows Server VM with a bundled SQL Express instance, or does the PAM module require a separate licensing activation call home during install that would block air-gapped provisioning?

Scoring surface — Find a Solution Engineer or API Lead on the Devolutions Server team. Does the Devolutions Server REST API (or PowerShell module) expose read endpoints to verify PAM checkout state — specifically, can we programmatically query whether a privileged account has been checked out, an approval workflow was triggered, and a session was recorded — so we can auto-score learner tasks in a lab?

NFR licensing — Find the Director of Training or Director of Partner Enablement. For hands-on training labs that spin up and tear down Devolutions Server + PAM instances per learner session, does Devolutions offer NFR or education-tier licenses that permit ephemeral, multi-tenant lab deployments without per-seat production licensing, and what is the maximum concurrent instance count under such a license?

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: Most teams we talk to underestimate how much their security posture depends on proper PAM configuration—we've found that hands-on practice with credential rotation, access workflows, and audit trails actually sticks way better than documentation alone, especially when your team is managing hundreds of endpoints.

Devolutions PAM integrations with your existing infrastructure can get complex fast, and we've seen adoption rates jump significantly when people can actually walk through a deployment scenario in a lab before they're doing it in production.

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Devolutions builds PAM and IT security solutions — likely trains customers and partners on complex credential management workflows, making hands-on lab scenarios a natural fit for their academy

Company operates a structured partner enablement program (based on Director of Partner Enablement role), suggesting they invest in certification and training infrastructure to scale partner competency
